Prediction distribution per cell

Urdu prompts collapse to a single label. English prompts spread across all three classes.

Paired McNemar Tests — English vs Urdu

b = EN correct & UR wrong  |  c = UR correct & EN wrong  |  b > c means English is better

What this means

A significant result (p < 0.05) means the difference between English and Urdu prompts is statistically real — not due to chance. All 4 significant results favour English. The 2 non-significant pairs (qwen05 few-shot, qwen15 CoT) are cases where both conditions fail badly, just in different ways — not genuine parity.
